desc: El Cotillo is a low-rise surf-and-lagoon village on Fuerteventura’s northwest coast, ideal for travelers who want beaches, wind sports, and a quieter Canary Islands base.
hls: Known for sheltered lagoons south of town and surf beaches to the north with stronger swell.
Warm, dry weather is consistent across the year, with very low rainfall compared with mainland Europe.
Wind increases through late spring and summer, great for watersports but less ideal for fully calm beach days.
Summer school-holiday weeks push accommodation demand highest despite stable climate year-round.
Works especially well for a 4-7 night beach/surf reset without heavy nightlife focus.
Easy to combine with Corralejo, volcano landscapes, and island road-trip routes.

todo.name: Old harbor + sunset promenade
todo.desc: Start with the fishing harbor and waterfront promenade for orientation, then return at sunset for calmer light and views.

todo.name: Lagoons beach day (La Concha area)
todo.desc: Use the sheltered lagoon beaches for relaxed swimming and family-friendly water time at mid-to-high tide.

todo.name: North-shore surf session
todo.desc: Book a lesson or guided surf block on the exposed north beaches where conditions suit stronger wave riding.

todo.name: Tostón lighthouse + coast drive
todo.desc: Drive or cycle to Faro del Tostón and nearby coves for short cliff walks and photo stops.

todo.name: Local seafood dinner circuit
todo.desc: Plan one meal around Canarian seafood and papas arrugadas in small harbor-side restaurants.

todo.name: Day trip to Corralejo dunes
todo.desc: Add the Corralejo Natural Park dunes for contrasting desert-like scenery and broad Atlantic viewpoints.

wifi.note: Good enough for routine remote work in apartments and cafés, though speeds vary by building and peak occupancy.

prac.fltNote: Fuerteventura has regular UK nonstop service (to FUE) from multiple airports; fares usually rise in school-holiday peaks (especially summer and Christmas).
prac.currency: Euro (EUR). Card payments are broadly accepted, with limited cash useful for small bars or kiosks.
prac.lang: Spanish is official; English is widely understood in tourism businesses around El Cotillo and Corralejo.
prac.visa: Spain/Schengen entry rules apply; verify passport validity and Schengen stay allowances for your nationality.
bestFor: Quiet beach villages over nightlife hubs
alerts: Atlantic currents can be strong on exposed beaches—swim only where conditions are appropriate.
Rental car availability can tighten in peak weeks; book early for best rates.
Windy days are common in summer, so plan at least one sheltered-lagoon fallback beach option.

sweet: March to June and September to November often give the best balance of warm weather, manageable crowds, and solid flight value outside peak school-holiday pressure.
